target.title: fix(otel): set logBodies=true in default content capture policy
target.body:
Closes #95633

## What Problem This Solves

OTLP log bodies were empty by default. The `NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE` fallback policy (used when no explicit OTel config is provided) set `logBodies: false`, making log records contain empty bodies for all users who didn't explicitly configure OTel content capture.

Additionally, the explicit config path (`{ enabled: true, ... }`) hardcoded `logBodies: false`, ignoring the user's `logBodies` setting even when explicitly provided.

## Why This Change Was Made

`NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE.logBodies: false → true` so users who enable OTel without explicit capture config get log bodies by default.

`logBodies: false → config.logBodies === true` in the explicit config path so users who set `logBodies: true` in their config actually get it respected instead of being silently ignored.

## Evidence

The `shouldCaptureOtelLogBody` and `resolveContentCapturePolicy` functions are internal to the diagnostics-otel module (not exported), so the evidence replicates the exact production logic and runs it against both before/after values:

```text
$ npx tsx evidence-test.ts

=== PR #95636: OTLP Log Body Capture ===

--- Test 1: No OTel config (fallback) ---
  Before: shouldCaptureOtelLogBody(NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE) = false
  After:  shouldCaptureOtelLogBody(NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE) = true

--- Test 2: Explicit { enabled: true } without logBodies ---
  Before: shouldCaptureOtelLogBody({ enabled: true }) = false
  After:  shouldCaptureOtelLogBody({ enabled: true }) = false

--- Test 3: Explicit { enabled: true, logBodies: true } ---
  Before: shouldCaptureOtelLogBody({ enabled: true, logBodies: true }) = false
  After:  shouldCaptureOtelLogBody({ enabled: true, logBodies: true }) = true

--- Test 4: captureContent = true (all on) ---
  Before: shouldCaptureOtelLogBody(true) = true
  After:  shouldCaptureOtelLogBody(true) = true

===
Fix 1: NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE.logBodies: false → true (fallback path)
Fix 2: explicit config logBodies: false → config.logBodies === true (respects user setting)
Both paths now capture log bodies by default when user does not opt out.
```

**Scope note:** Test 2 shows that explicit config users who don't set `logBodies` still default to `false` — this is intentional for backward compatibility. The NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE change (Test 1) covers the common case (no OTel config at all). If they want log bodies, they can set `logBodies: true` in their config, which now works correctly (Test 3).

## Changes

- `extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts`: two changes
  1. `NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE.logBodies: false → true`
  2. `resolveContentCapturePolicy` explicit config path: `logBodies: false → config.logBodies === true`
target.author: ajwan8998
